from the one succeeding it. Having ascertained the ascendant
we have to fix the actual time.
I
A planet located alone in a particular hemisphere acquires
a special importance and prominence. By being situated thus
it is vested with a peculiar emphasis. To begin with, let us
take Mars. To this planet practical things are more important
than ideal ones; thereby it refers to activity, initiatives, and
originality.

The first chart is that of Karl Marx. Here is a northern
hemisphere emphasis, in so far as the planets are concerned.
Life for him must have been an intensely subjective one torn
by an inner or spiritual conflict. As in the case of Luther, Marx
too was opposed to superficial religion, outward pomp and
extravagance and vanity.
